Feylost.entries.4.entries.2.rows.2.1: Good music makes me weep like a baby.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.2.rows.3.1: Wherever I go, I try to bring a little of the warmth and tranquility of home with me.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.2.rows.4.1: I have never lost my childlike sense of wonder.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.2.rows.5.1: When I have a new idea, I get wildly excited about it until I come up with another, better idea.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.2.rows.6.1: I live by my own set of weird and wonderful rules.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.2.rows.7.1: I can't bring myself to trust most adults.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.3.caption: Ideals
Feylost.entries.4.entries.3.rows.0.1: Friendship. I never leave a friend behind. (Good)
Feylost.entries.4.entries.3.rows.1.1: Empathy. No creature should be made to suffer. (Good)
Feylost.entries.4.entries.3.rows.2.1: Wanderlust. I prefer to take the less traveled path. (Chaotic)
Feylost.entries.4.entries.3.rows.3.1: Changeability. Change is good, which is why I live by an ever-changing set of rules. (Chaotic)
Feylost.entries.4.entries.3.rows.4.1: Honor. A deal is a deal, and I would never break one. (Lawful)
Feylost.entries.4.entries.3.rows.5.1: Rule of Three. Everything in the multiverse happens in threes. I see the "rule of three" everywhere. (Lawful)
Feylost.entries.4.entries.3.rows.6.1: Obsession. I won't let go of a grudge. (Evil)
Feylost.entries.4.entries.3.rows.7.1: Greed. I will do whatever it takes to get what I want, regardless of the harm it might cause. (Evil)
Feylost.entries.4.entries.4.caption: Bonds
Feylost.entries.4.entries.4.rows.0.1: I would never break my word.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.4.rows.1.1: I find magic in all its forms to be compelling. The more magical a place, the more I am drawn to it.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.4.rows.2.1: I do what I can to protect the natural world.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.4.rows.3.1: A trusted friend is the most important thing in the multiverse to me.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.4.rows.4.1: I can't bring myself to harm a Fey creature, either because I consider myself one or because I fear the repercussions.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.4.rows.5.1: The Witchlight Carnival feels like home to me.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.4.rows.6.1: I'm drawn to the Feywild and long to return there, if only for a short while.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.4.rows.7.1: I feel indebted to {@creature Mister Witch|WBtW} and {@creature Mister Light|WBtW} for giving me a home and a purpose.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.5.caption: Flaws
Feylost.entries.4.entries.5.rows.0.1: I easily lose track of time. My poor sense of time means I'm always late.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.5.rows.1.1: I think the whole multiverse is out to get me.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.5.rows.2.1: I'm always operating under a tight timeline, and I'm obsessed with keeping everything on schedule.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.5.rows.3.1: I'm a kleptomaniac who covets shiny, sparkling treasure.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.5.rows.4.1: I'm forgetful. Sometimes I can't remember even the simplest things.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.5.rows.5.1: I never give away anything for free and always expect something in return.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.5.rows.6.1: I have many vices and tend to indulge them.
Feylost.entries.4.entries.5.rows.7.1: I'm always changing my mind—well, almost always.
Feylost.entries.4.name: Character Traits
Feylost.fluff.0: You grew up in the Feywild after disappearing from your home plane as a child. Perhaps you were spirited away by kindly Fey who thought you were destined for great things. Perhaps you stumbled through a fey crossing by chance during a twilight stroll in the woods. Perhaps you were kidnapped by evil Fey but escaped from their clutches. Whatever the manner of your disappearance, you gradually fell under the Feywild's spell and learned a little about the nature of the mercurial tricksters that dwell there.
Feylost.fluff.1: When you finally returned to your home plane, you did not come back unchanged. You are haunted by the fact that the Feywild—a mirror world hidden behind a mere twist of perception—is only a hair's breadth away. Although your memories of the Feywild grow fainter with each passing day, your heart swells with a mixture of fear and joy at the prospect of one day venturing back to the Plane of Faerie—your home away from home.
Feylost.name: Feylost
Fisher.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Fisher.entries.0.items.1.name: Languages:
Fisher.entries.0.items.2.entry: {@item Fishing tackle|phb}, a {@item net|phb}, a favorite fishing lure or oiled leather wading boots, a set of {@item traveler's clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 10 gp
Fisher.entries.0.items.2.name: Equipment:
Fisher.entries.1.entries.0: You gain advantage on ability checks made using fishing tackle. If you have access to a body of water that sustains marine life, you can maintain a moderate lifestyle while working as a fisher, and you can catch enough food to feed yourself and up to ten other people each day.
Fisher.entries.1.name: Feature: Harvest the Water
Fisher.entries.2.entries.0: You can tell a compelling tale, whether tall or true, to impress and entertain others. Once a day, you can tell your story to willing listeners. At the DM's discretion, a number of those listeners become friendly toward you; this is not a magical effect, and continued amicability on their part depends on your actions. You can roll on the following table to help determine the theme of your tale or choose one that best fits your character. Alternatively, work with your DM to create your own fishing tale.
Fisher.entries.2.entries.1.rows.0.1: Lobster Wrestling. You fought in hand-to-hand combat with an immense lobster.
Fisher.entries.2.entries.1.rows.1.1: It Dragged the Boat. You nearly caught a fish of monstrous size that pulled your boat for miles.
Fisher.entries.2.entries.1.rows.2.1: Fins of Pure Gold. You caught a sea animal whose fins were made of pure gold, but another fisher stole it.
Fisher.entries.2.entries.1.rows.3.1: Ghost Fish. You are haunted by a ghostly fish that only you can see.
Fisher.entries.2.entries.1.rows.4.1: Nemesis Clam. A large clam containing a pearl the size of your head claimed one of your fingers before jetting away; one day, you'll find that clam.
Fisher.entries.2.entries.1.rows.5.1: It Swallowed the Sun. You once saw a fish leap from the water and turn day into night.
Fisher.entries.2.entries.1.rows.6.1: Dive into the Abyss. You found yourself in an underwater cave leading to the Abyss, and your luck has been sour ever since.
Fisher.entries.2.entries.1.rows.7.1: Love Story. You fell in love with a creature of pure water, but your brief romance ended tragically.
Fisher.entries.2.name: Fishing Tale
Fisher.entries.3.entries.0: Fishers succeed only if they spend time at their jobs. As such, most fishers have a strong work ethic, and they admire others who earn their living honestly. Fishers tend to be superstitious, forming attachments to particular fishing lures or special fishing spots. They have a connection to the bodies of water in which they fish, and they think poorly of those whose actions adversely affect their livelihood.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.1.caption: Fisher Personality Traits
Fisher.entries.3.entries.1.rows.0.1: I am unmoved by the wrath of nature.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.1.rows.1.1: My friends are my crew; we sink or float together.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.1.rows.2.1: I need long stretches of quiet to clear my head.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.1.rows.3.1: Rich folk don't know the satisfaction of hard work.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.1.rows.4.1: I laugh heartily, feel deeply, and fear nothing.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.1.rows.5.1: I work hard; nature offers no handouts.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.1.rows.6.1: I dislike bargaining; state your price and mean it.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.1.rows.7.1: Luck favors me, and I take risks others might not.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.2.caption: Fisher Ideals
Fisher.entries.3.entries.2.rows.0.1: {@b Camaraderie.} Good people make even the longest voyage bearable. (Good)
Fisher.entries.3.entries.2.rows.1.1: {@b Luck.} Our luck depends on respecting its rules—now throw this salt over your shoulder. (Lawful)
Fisher.entries.3.entries.2.rows.2.1: {@b Daring.} The richest bounty goes to those who risk everything. (Chaotic)
Fisher.entries.3.entries.2.rows.3.1: {@b Plunder.} Take all that you can and leave nothing for the scavengers. (Evil)
Fisher.entries.3.entries.2.rows.4.1: {@b Balance.} Do not fish the same spot twice in a row; suppress your greed, and nature will reward you. (Neutral)
Fisher.entries.3.entries.2.rows.5.1: {@b Hard Work.} No wave can move a soul hard at work. (Any)
Fisher.entries.3.entries.3.caption: Fisher Bonds
Fisher.entries.3.entries.3.rows.0.1: I lost something important in the deep sea, and I intend to find it.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.3.rows.1.1: Someone else's greed destroyed my livelihood, and I will be compensated.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.3.rows.2.1: I will fish the many famous waters of this land.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.3.rows.3.1: The gods saved me during a terrible storm, and I will honor their gift.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.3.rows.4.1: My destiny awaits me at the bottom of a particular pond in the Feywild.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.3.rows.5.1: I must repay my village's debt.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.4.caption: Fisher Flaws
Fisher.entries.3.entries.4.rows.0.1: I am judgmental, especially of those I deem homebodies or otherwise lazy.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.4.rows.1.1: I become depressed and anxious if I'm away from the sea too long.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.4.rows.2.1: I have lived a hard life and find it difficult to empathize with others.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.4.rows.3.1: I am inclined to tell long-winded stories at inopportune times.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.4.rows.4.1: I work hard, but I play harder.
Fisher.entries.3.entries.4.rows.5.1: I am obsessed with catching an elusive aquatic beast, often to the detriment of other pursuits.
Fisher.entries.3.name: Suggested Characteristics
Fisher.name: Fisher
Flaming Fist Mercenary.entries.0.items.4: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Mace|XPHB}, {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Fine Clothes|XPHB}, {@item Manacles|XPHB}, {@item Portable Ram|XPHB}, 4 GP; or (B) 50 GP
Flaming Fist Mercenary.fluff.0: The chief law enforcement branch of Baldur's Gate is the Flaming Fist, a brawny mercenary guild led by the city's grand duke. You once served as a Flaming Fist, where you learned how to preempt trouble with your intimidating stare and, when necessary, absorb deadly blows. Flaming Fist mercenaries, active or retired, are known as some of the toughest, most resilient warriors along the Sword Coast, and you seek to maintain that reputation.
Flaming Fist Mercenary.name: Flaming Fist Mercenary
Folk Hero.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Folk Hero.entries.0.items.1.entry: One type of {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools}, {@filter vehicles (land)|items|source=phb;dmg|miscellaneous=mundane|type=vehicle (land)}
Folk Hero.entries.0.items.1.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Folk Hero.entries.0.items.2.entry: A set of {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools} (one of your choice), a {@item shovel|phb}, an {@item iron pot|phb}, a set of {@item common clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 10 gp
Folk Hero.entries.0.items.2.name: Equipment:
Folk Hero.entries.1.entries.0: Since you come from the ranks of the common folk, you fit in among them with ease. You can find a place to hide, rest, or recuperate among other commoners, unless you have shown yourself to be a danger to them. They will shield you from the law or anyone else searching for you, though they will not risk their lives for you.
Folk Hero.entries.1.name: Feature: Rustic Hospitality
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.0: You previously pursued a simple profession among the peasantry, perhaps as a farmer, miner, servant, shepherd, woodcutter, or gravedigger. But something happened that set you on a different path and marked you for greater things. Choose or randomly determine a defining event that marked you as a hero of the people.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.0.1: I stood up to a tyrant's agents.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.1.1: I saved people during a natural disaster.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.2.1: I stood alone against a terrible monster.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.3.1: I stole from a corrupt merchant to help the poor.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.4.1: I led a militia to fight off an invading army.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.5.1: I broke into a tyrant's castle and stole weapons to arm the people.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.6.1: I trained the peasantry to use farming implements as weapons against a tyrant's soldiers.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.7.1: A lord rescinded an unpopular decree after I led a symbolic act of protest against it.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.8.1: A celestial, fey, or similar creature gave me a blessing or revealed my secret origin.
Folk Hero.entries.2.entries.1.rows.9.1: Recruited into a lord's army, I rose to leadership and was commended for my heroism.
Folk Hero.entries.2.name: Specialty
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.0: A folk hero is one of the common people, for better or for worse. Most folk heroes look on their humble origins as a virtue, not a shortcoming, and their home communities remain very important to them.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.1.rows.0.1: I judge people by their actions, not their words.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.1.rows.1.1: If someone is in trouble, I'm always ready to lend help.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.1.rows.2.1: When I set my mind to something, I follow through no matter what gets in my way.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.1.rows.3.1: I have a strong sense of fair play and always try to find the most equitable solution to arguments.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.1.rows.4.1: I'm confident in my own abilities and do what I can to instill confidence in others.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.1.rows.5.1: Thinking is for other people. I prefer action.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.1.rows.6.1: I misuse long words in an attempt to sound smarter.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.1.rows.7.1: I get bored easily. When am I going to get on with my destiny?
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.2.rows.0.1: Respect. People deserve to be treated with dignity and respect. (Good)
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.2.rows.1.1: Fairness. No one should get preferential treatment before the law, and no one is above the law. (Lawful)
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.2.rows.2.1: Freedom. Tyrants must not be allowed to oppress the people. (Chaotic)
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.2.rows.3.1: Might. If I become strong, I can take what I want—what I deserve. (Evil)
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.2.rows.4.1: Sincerity. There's no good in pretending to be something I'm not. (Neutral)
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.2.rows.5.1: Destiny. Nothing and no one can steer me away from my higher calling. (Any)
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.3.rows.0.1: I have a family, but I have no idea where they are. One day, I hope to see them again.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.3.rows.1.1: I worked the land, I love the land, and I will protect the land.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.3.rows.2.1: A proud noble once gave me a horrible beating, and I will take my revenge on any bully I encounter.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.3.rows.3.1: My tools are symbols of my past life, and I carry them so that I will never forget my roots.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.3.rows.4.1: I protect those who cannot protect themselves.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.3.rows.5.1: I wish my childhood sweetheart had come with me to pursue my destiny.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.4.rows.0.1: The tyrant who rules my land will stop at nothing to see me killed.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.4.rows.1.1: I'm convinced of the significance of my destiny, and blind to my shortcomings and the risk of failure.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.4.rows.2.1: The people who knew me when I was young know my shameful secret, so I can never go home again.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.4.rows.3.1: I have a weakness for the vices of the city, especially hard drink.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.4.rows.4.1: Secretly, I believe that things would be better if I were a tyrant lording over the land.
Folk Hero.entries.3.entries.4.rows.5.1: I have trouble trusting in my allies.
Folk Hero.entries.3.name: Suggested Characteristics
Folk Hero.name: Folk Hero
Choose A or B: (A) {@item Dagger|XPHB}, {@item Spear|XPHB}, {@item Gaming Set|XPHB} (same as above), {@item Robe|XPHB}, 10 GP; or (B) 50 GP
Free Swords Mercenary.name: Free Swords Mercenary
Gambler.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Gambler.entries.0.items.1.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Gambler.entries.0.items.2.name: Languages:
Gambler.entries.0.items.3.entry: One {@filter gaming set|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=gaming set}, a lucky charm, a set of {@item fine clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 15 gp
Gambler.entries.0.items.3.name: Equipment:
Gambler.entries.1.entries.0: Odds and probability are your bread and butter. During downtime activities that involve games of chance or figuring odds on the best plan, you can get a solid sense of which choice is likely the best one and which opportunities seem too good to be true, at the DM's determination.
Gambler.entries.1.name: Feature: Never Tell Me the Odds
Gambler.entries.2.entries.0: Some gamble out of necessity. Others do so out of boredom. Still others become addicted to the thrill of winning or losing everything on a turn of fortune. For some, gambling is less a matter of chance and more a matter of seeking every advantage to ensure the outcome. The best gamblers can lose everything, and the worst gamblers sometimes win. Regardless, you can always tell gamblers by the look in their eyes. Lady Luck haunts them.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.1.rows.0.1: I plan for every contingency. Leave nothing to chance!
Gambler.entries.2.entries.1.rows.1.1: Every copper wants to be a silver. Each bet is an opportunity.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.1.rows.2.1: I'm one of Lady Luck's favored. Anything I try is destined to succeed.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.1.rows.3.1: I've lost so much to gambling that I refuse to spend money on anything anymore.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.1.rows.4.1: Nothing is certain. Planning is a coward's act.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.1.rows.5.1: I can't be sure who I've swindled, cheated, or defeated, so I keep a low profile in public.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.1.rows.6.1: The perfect bet is out there somewhere. I just have to keep my eyes open.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.1.rows.7.1: I have beaten my addiction, but all it takes is one weak moment and I'll be back at the card table.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.2.rows.0.1: Knowledge. Knowledge is power, and knowing which horse to back is the key to success. (Any)
Gambler.entries.2.entries.2.rows.1.1: Fate. Whatever happens is fated, regardless of any planning or striving. (Lawful)
Gambler.entries.2.entries.2.rows.2.1: Bravery. If you want to succeed, you have to take risks. (Chaotic)
Gambler.entries.2.entries.2.rows.3.1: Survival. You can't win if you're dead. Live to fight another day—when the odds might be more in your favor. (Any)
Gambler.entries.2.entries.2.rows.4.1: Reliability. When I was in need, I was able to rely on others. Now I want to be the one others rely on. (Good)
Gambler.entries.2.entries.2.rows.5.1: Victory. Winning is the real measure of a person. In the end, the only thing that matters is the scoreboard. (Evil)
Gambler.entries.2.entries.3.rows.0.1: One person in particular owes me a lot of money, and I need to keep them alive if I want to be repaid.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.3.rows.1.1: I'm loyal to the friend or family member who taught me how to gamble.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.3.rows.2.1: The person who saved me from my gambling addiction is the only reason I'm alive today.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.3.rows.3.1: A patron once fronted me money in exchange for a percentage of my winnings. I owe them a debt of gratitude. And a lot of cash.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.3.rows.4.1: A criminal syndicate I once played for isn't happy I left the game, and its enforcers are looking for me.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.3.rows.5.1: Urchins once helped me find marks for my games. Now I'm driven to help them escape the streets.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.4.rows.0.1: I don't know when to quit. Especially when everyone else is telling me to.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.4.rows.1.1: I save my sympathy for my friends, and I have no friends.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.4.rows.2.1: You think we're in trouble now? Let me tell you how bad things are likely to get!
Gambler.entries.2.entries.4.rows.3.1: You can loan me a little, right? I've got a sure thing. I'll double your money, guaranteed.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.4.rows.4.1: I was once a terribly flawed person, like you. Let me tell you how you can save yourself.
Gambler.entries.2.entries.4.rows.5.1: I'm a great gambler. I'm just bad at math and logic.
Gambler.entries.2.name: Suggested Characteristics
Gambler.name: Gambler
Gate Urchin.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Gate Urchin.entries.0.items.1.entry: {@item Thieves' tools|phb}, one type of {@filter musical instrument|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=instrument}
Gate Urchin.entries.0.items.1.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Gate Urchin.entries.0.items.2.entry: A battered alms box, a {@filter musical instrument|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=instrument}, a cast-off military jacket, cap, or scarf, a set of {@item common clothes|phb}, a belt {@item pouch|phb}, and 10 gp
Gate Urchin.entries.0.items.2.name: Equipment:
Gate Urchin.entries.1.name: Lifestyle
Gate Urchin.entries.2.entries.0: All traffic into and out of the City of Trade passes through the Hillsfar Gate, making it the ideal place for the destitute to gather to panhandle, busk, gossip, and pick pockets. You grew up on the streets in the shadow of that great steel edifice, which houses both Red Plumes and Guild Mages. Though you may have moved on, you still have friends among them, and that life has had a lasting impact on you.
Gate Urchin.entries.2.name: Overview
Gate Urchin.entries.3.entries.0: You made a number of friends among the Red Plumes and the Mage's Guild when you lived at the Hillsfar Gate. They remember you fondly and help you in little ways when they can. You can invoke their assistance in and around Hillsfar to obtain food, as well as simple equipment for temporary use. You can also invoke it to gain access to the low-security areas of their garrisons, halls, and encampments. Note: This feature is a variant of the Soldier feature.
Gate Urchin.entries.3.name: Feature: Red Plume and Mage Guild Contacts
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.0.rows.0.1: I appreciate the simple things in life. a song, a warm meal, a sunny day. I don't need any more.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.0.rows.1.1: My problems are always caused by others. I'm never to blame.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.0.rows.2.1: I am afraid I could wind up back on the streets any day.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.0.rows.4.1: I see people as marks for a con and have difficulty feeling true empathy for them.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.0.rows.5.1: I have a real flair for matchmaking. I can find anyone a spouse!
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.0.rows.6.1: I think money is the true measure of appreciation and affection. Everything else is talk or an act.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.0.rows.7.1: I don't like having a lot of stuff, just a few simple things I need. I don't like being tied down and tend to leave things behind when I don't need them anymore.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.1.rows.0.1: Loyal. I never rat out any of my friends, even when the Red Plumes or the Rogues Guild ask. (Lawful)
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.1.rows.1.1: Adventurous. I don't like doing the same thing every day. I crave variety. (Chaotic)
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.1.rows.2.1: Strong. Only the strong survive. I respect those who are strong and powerful. (Any)
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.1.rows.3.1: Witty. Brains are better than brawn. I rely on my wits and respect others who do the same. (Any)
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.1.rows.4.1: Honest. Others can do what they want, but I won't lie or steal, even to feed my family. (Good)
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.1.rows.5.1: Ungrateful. Those who give, only do it to make themselves feel better. I steal from them. (Evil)
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.2.rows.0.1: The Joydancers of Lliira gave me my instrument when I really needed food. I hate them for that.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.2.rows.1.1: Busking has taught me to love music above all else.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.2.rows.2.1: The Rogues Guild spared me when I did a job without cutting them in. I owe them a great debt.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.2.rows.3.1: I know people hate the Red Plumes, but some of them were really good to me. I help Red Plumes whenever I can, and I respect them. They're just doing what they have to do to get by in this world.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.2.rows.4.1: I will be wealthy some day. My descendants will live in comfort and style.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.2.rows.5.1: I know how hard life on the streets is. I do everything I can for those who have less than me.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.3.rows.0.1: Though I no longer live at the Gate, I am still always concerned about where I will get my next meal.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.3.rows.1.1: Years of thieving have become habit. I sometimes steal from strangers without thinking about it.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.3.rows.2.1: I am ashamed of my origins. I pretend I am higher-born and fear others will find out the truth.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.3.rows.3.1: I think people who grew up in houses are soft, spoiled, and ungrateful. I frequently tell them so.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.3.rows.4.1: I am still very uncomfortable wearing nice clothes, sleeping in a warm bed, and eating fine food.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.entries.3.rows.5.1: I do not trust anyone who has not had a hard life.
Gate Urchin.entries.4.name: Suggested Characteristics
Gate Urchin.name: Gate Urchin
Gate Warden.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Gate Warden.entries.0.items.1.entry: Two of your choice (Abyssal, Celestial, or Infernal recommended)
Gate Warden.entries.0.items.1.name: Languages:
Gate Warden.entries.0.items.2.entry: A ring of keys to unknown locks, a blank {@item book|PHB}, an {@item ink pen|PHB} or quill, a bottle of black ink, a set of {@item traveler's clothes|PHB}, and a {@item pouch|PHB} containing 10 gp
Gate Warden.entries.0.items.2.name: Equipment:
Gate Warden.entries.1.entries.0: Living in a gate-town or a similar location steeped you in planar energy. You gain the {@feat Scion of the Outer Planes|SatO} feat. In addition, you know where to find free, modest lodging and food in the community you grew up in.
Gate Warden.entries.1.name: Feature: Planar Infusion
Gate Warden.entries.2.entries.0: The influence of an Outer Plane shapes your perspective. The Gate Warden Personality Traits table suggests traits you might adopt for your character.
Gate Warden.entries.2.entries.1.rows.0.1: Strange events and otherworldly creatures don't faze me.
Gate Warden.entries.2.entries.1.rows.1.1: I think in terms of exchange: something for something, nothing for nothing.
Gate Warden.entries.2.entries.1.rows.2.1: I speak with an unusual cadence.
Gate Warden.entries.2.entries.1.rows.3.1: I pepper my speech with words or curses borrowed from planar languages.
Gate Warden.entries.2.entries.1.rows.4.1: I've seen enough to know that you can't take anyone at face value, so I scrutinize everyone.
Gate Warden.entries.2.entries.1.rows.5.1: I have a superstitious habit I picked up from my gate-town, such as touching iron when I'm nervous or arranging objects in a specific order.
Gate Warden.entries.2.name: Suggested Characteristics
Gate Warden.entries.3.entries.0: Those who dwell for an extended time near a permanent portal to another plane absorb the essence radiating from the realm beyond. Those influenced by the same plane share similarities in behavior and even physical appearance.
When you make your character, you can roll once on the {@item Gate Warden Trinket|SatO|Gate Warden Trinkets} table, instead of on the {@item Trinket|PHB|Trinkets} table in the Player's Handbook, for your starting trinket.
Gate Warden.entries.3.entries.1.name: Gate Warden Trinkets
Gate Warden.entries.3.name: Building a Gate Warden Character
Gate Warden.fluff.0: You spent a significant amount of time somewhere influenced by intense planar forces or a portal to another plane of existence, such as one of the gatetowns in the Outlands. You're accustomed to experiences that would leave others reeling in terror or enraptured by otherworldly beauty, and you're as comfortable dealing with Celestials and Fiends as you are with the vendors in town (who might be one and the same).
Gate Warden.name: Gate Warden
Genie Touched.entries.0.items.0.name: Ability Scores:
Genie Touched.entries.0.items.1.name: Feat:
Genie Touched.entries.0.items.2.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Genie Touched.entries.0.items.3.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Genie Touched.entries.0.items.4.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Light Hammer|XPHB}, {@item Glassblower's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Fine Clothes|XPHB}, {@item Lamp|XPHB}, {@item Oil|XPHB} (3 flasks), {@item Waterskin|XPHB}, 2 GP; or (B) 50 GP
Genie Touched.entries.0.items.4.name: Equipment:
Genie Touched.fluff.0: Although genies no longer rule Calimshan, genie magic is still common in your homeland. Perhaps you inadvertently summoned a djinni from a magic lamp, or maybe you came upon an oasis guarded by a marid. A dao might have saved you from a landslide, or you bargained with an efreeti for fleeting wealth. However your fate intersected with that of a genie, the experience left you with a keen eye, a silver tongue, and more than a touch of magic.
Genie Touched.name: Genie Touched
Giant Foundling.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Giant Foundling.entries.0.items.1.entry: Giant and one other language of your choice
Giant Foundling.entries.0.items.1.name: Languages:
Giant Foundling.entries.0.items.2.entry: A {@item backpack|phb}, a set of {@item traveler's clothes|phb}, a small stone or sprig that reminds you of home, and a {@item pouch|phb} containing 10 gp
Giant Foundling.entries.0.items.2.name: Equipment:
Giant Foundling.entries.1.entries.0: How you came to live among colossal creatures is up to you to determine, but the Foundling Origin table suggests a variety of possibilities.
Giant Foundling.entries.1.entries.1.rows: You were found as a baby by a family of nomadic giants who raised you as one of their own.
A family of stone giants rescued you when you fell into a mountain chasm, and you have lived with them underground ever since.
You were lost or abandoned as a child in a jungle that teemed with ravenous dinosaurs. There, you found an equally lost frost giant; together, you survived.
Your farm was crushed and your family killed in a battle between warring groups of giants. Racked with guilt over the destruction, a sympathetic giant soldier promised to care for you.
After you had a series of strange dreams as a child, your superstitious parents sent you to study with a powerful but aloof storm giant oracle.
While playing hide-and-seek with your friends, you stumbled into the castle of a cloud giant, who immediately adopted you.
Giant Foundling.entries.1.name: Origin Stories
Giant Foundling.entries.2.entries.0: Your life among giants has given you a unique perspective. Though you are unusually large for your kind, you're no larger than a giant child, so you might be very mindful of your size.
Giant Foundling.entries.2.name: Building a Giant Foundling Character
Giant Foundling.entries.3.entries.0: You gain the {@feat Strike of the Giants|BGG} feat.
Giant Foundling.entries.3.name: Feature: Strike of the Giants
Giant Foundling.entries.4.entries.0: The Giant Foundling Personality Traits table suggests a variety of traits you might adopt for your character.
Giant Foundling.entries.4.entries.1.rows: What I lack in stature compared to giants, I make up for with sheer spite.
I insist on being taken seriously as a full-grown adult. Nobody talks down to me!
Crowded spaces make me uncomfortable. I'd much rather be in an open field than a bustling tavern.
I embrace my shorter stature. It helps me stay unnoticed—and underestimated.
Every avalanche begins as a single pebble.
The world always feels too big, and I'm afraid I'll never find my place in it.
Giant Foundling.entries.4.name: Suggested Characteristics
Giant Foundling.fluff.0: Though you aren't a Giant, you grew up among giants. Maybe you were an orphan taken in by a sympathetic family of stone giants who raised you as one of their own. Or perhaps you lived in a lost prehistoric pocket of the world, surrounded by giants and fearsome behemoths or hulking dinosaurs.
Giant Foundling.fluff.1: Something about your environment—perhaps the food or water that sustained you, elemental magic inherent in the site of your home, or some verdant blessing of growth placed on you—caused you to grow to a remarkable size for your kind. With the aid of this magic, you have learned how to embody the might of giants. You are used to moving through a world much bigger than you, and that is reflected in your skills, attitude, and perspective on life.
Giant Foundling.name: Giant Foundling
Golgari Agent.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Golgari Agent.entries.0.items.1.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Golgari Agent.entries.0.items.2.entry: Choose one of Elvish, Giant, or Kraul
Golgari Agent.entries.0.items.2.name: Languages:
Golgari Agent.entries.0.items.3.entry: A Golgari insignia, a {@item poisoner's kit|phb}, a pet beetle or spider, a set of {@item common clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 10 gp worth of mixed coins
Golgari Agent.entries.0.items.3.name: Equipment:
Golgari Agent.entries.1.entries.0: You know hidden, underground pathways that you can use to bypass crowds, obstacles, and observation as you move through the city. When you aren't in combat, you and companions you lead can travel between any two locations in the city twice as fast as your speed would normally allow. The paths of the undercity are haunted by dangers that rarely brave the light of the surface world, so your journey isn't guaranteed to be safe.
Golgari Agent.entries.1.name: Feature: Undercity Paths
Golgari Agent.entries.2.entries.0: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature}
Golgari Agent.entries.2.entries.1: For you, the spells on the Golgari Guild Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class. (If you are a multiclass character with multiple spell lists, these spells are added to all of them.)
Golgari Agent.entries.2.entries.2.rows.2: {@spell protection from poison}, {@spell ray of enfeeblement}, {@spell spider climb}
Golgari Agent.entries.2.entries.3: Golgari magic is often accompanied by a sickly green glow and a rotting stench.
Golgari Agent.entries.2.name: Golgari Guild Spells
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.0: Members of the Golgari Swarm are unmistakably products of the undercity, ill at ease amid the comforts of civilization. They bring about the same discomfort in others by reminding them of death's inevitable approach.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.1.rows.0: Remember, I could kill you in your sleep. Or put centipedes in your bedroll.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.1.rows.1: I like to remind people of their inevitable demise.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.1.rows.2: Sometimes I give voice to the whispers of the rot, which I hear but no one else does.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.1.rows.3: I do my best to discourage anyone from approaching or talking to me.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.1.rows.4: I have accepted my death. Hence, I don't fear it.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.1.rows.5: Like roots growing through stone, I am relentless and determined in my action.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.1.rows.6: I put my knowledge of anatomy to use by narrating the injuries my enemies suffer in grisly detail.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.1.rows.7: Like a wild animal, I lash out viciously when I'm provoked—and I'm easily provoked.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.2.rows.0: {@b Guild}. My guild is all that really matters. (Any)
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.2.rows.1: {@b Stoicism}. All of us are part of the cyclical march of nature, which will continue with or without us. (Neutral)
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.2.rows.2: {@b Nature}. The natural world is more important than the edifices of the city and civilization. (Neutral)
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.2.rows.3: {@b Interdependence}. We are all part of nature's web. (Lawful)
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.2.rows.4: {@b Ambition}. The time of Golgari ascendance is at hand, and I intend to have a prominent place in the new world order. (Evil)
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.2.rows.5: {@b Live and Let Live}. Meddling in the affairs of other guilds is a great way to get squashed like a bug. (Neutral)
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.3.rows.0: I cherish the finger of a family member who was {@condition petrified} by a medusa.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.3.rows.1: I have an identical twin who is as different from me as any person could be.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.3.rows.2: I want to lead one faction of the guild to a new position of dominance.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.3.rows.3: I love spending time in the moss-covered building where I took part in my first reclamation mission.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.3.rows.4: I found something in the sewer that must never come to light.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.3.rows.5: I am forever grateful to the reclaimer who found me floating facedown in the sewer, moments from death.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.4.rows.0: Death comes for us all, so you can't expect me to take care of someone who can't fight it off.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.4.rows.1: I assume that anyone outside the Golgari looks down on me.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.4.rows.2: I feel a need for revenge against those who enjoy the privilege of living above ground.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.4.rows.3: I don't bother to couch my opinions in flattering words.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.4.rows.4: I can't help but pocket any trinket or coin I come across, no matter how worthless.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.entries.4.rows.5: I'm convinced that I'm better and stronger than members of other guilds, isolated as they are from the realities of life and death.
Golgari Agent.entries.3.name: Suggested Characteristics
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.0: To the extent that the Golgari Swarm acts like a single organism, you are connected to every other member in some way or another. Convinced that the rest of the world is out to get you, you find it easy to form close bonds with your guild mates, and harder to make meaningful connections with others.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.1: Roll twice on the Golgari Contacts table (for an ally and a rival) and once on the Non-Golgari Contacts table.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.2.rows.0: One of my parents is an elite assassin, a member of the Ochran.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.2.rows.1: I learned combat from a kraul.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.2.rows.2: I know a medusa who is stationed in the guildhall.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.2.rows.3: I had a torrid romance with a spore druid responsible for a large rot farm.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.2.rows.4: There's a troll in a remote area of the undercity who seems to find me interesting—and who knows more than you'd think.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.2.rows.5: An elf lich is determined to see me become a lich someday, too.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.2.rows.6: A medusa decided it would be more fun to recruit me into the guild than to kill me.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.2.rows.7: I know a findbroker who can locate just about anything, for the right price.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.0: An Azorius arrester I literally pulled out of the gutter will do anything for me.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.1: Someone joined the Gruul in a battle against the Boros once, and the sergeant of that Boros squad would love to prove that it was me.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.2: I had a romance with a Dimir agent whom I still feed secrets to.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.3: Roll an additional Golgari contact; you can decide if the contact is an ally or a rival.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.4: I joined the Gruul in a battle against the Boros once, and the chief of that small clan thanks me for turning the tide.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.5: An Izzet scientist resents that I sold a scrapped invention I found in the sewer.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.6: My undercity explorations led me into an Orzhov vault, and a spirit thinks I stole something valuable.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.7: I found a baby beast and sold it to a Rakdos wrangler who remains grateful to me.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.8: A Selesnya druid and I share an interest in the same garden, and we have enjoyable arguments there.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.entries.3.rows.9: I regularly pick up refuse from beneath a Simic laboratory, and sometimes I talk to the researcher who dumps it there.
Golgari Agent.entries.4.name: Contacts
Golgari Agent.entries.5.entries.0: As part of the Golgari Swarm, you are a specialized instrument of the greater body. Your orders, when you have such, come from the guildmaster by way of his chancellors, who carry his messages throughout the guild. The swarm relies on you to advance the greater good by protecting some part, however small, of its teeming existence. That responsibility doesn't mean you're indispensable; your eventual death is part of your purpose and function, too, and you'll be replaced even as your body provides nutrients to further the swarm's growth.
Golgari Agent.entries.5.entries.1: A classic adventuring role for a member of the Golgari involves crawling through dungeon-like environments—the sewers and ancient vaults of the undercity—in search of treasures left behind by the dead. Sometimes you might be sent to find a specific item believed lost in a dangerous part of the undercity. At other times, you could be asked to collect samples of a specific fungus, retrieve a body floating in the muck of the sewers, or bring back whatever booty you can to help fill the swarm's coffers.
Golgari Agent.entries.5.entries.2: You might gain enough renown to become a member of the Ochran, assigned to a variety of tasks concerning thievery, assassination, or the protection of important figures in your guild. You might steal something because the guild needs it, or because its loss will bring harm to another guild, hastening that group's decline. You could be assigned to kill an outspoken and active enemy of the Golgari, such as an overzealous Boros captain whose raids into the undercity have approached dangerously close to the swarm's inner sanctum. Or you could serve as a bodyguard to one of Guildmaster Jarad's high chancellors, escorting this figure through the undercity while being ready to intervene at a moment's notice if things go wrong.
Golgari Agent.entries.5.entries.3: The shamans of the Golgari use their magic to accelerate the cycle of decay and regrowth. You might be sent to spread spores throughout an area that the Golgari want to claim as their territory or to convince the inhabitants of such a territory to abandon it. You might also contend with the ever-present threat of hostile monsters encroaching into Golgari-controlled regions.
Golgari Agent.entries.5.name: How Do I Fit In?
Golgari Agent.name: Golgari Agent
Grinner.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Grinner.entries.0.items.1.entry: One type of {@filter musical instrument|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=instrument}, {@item thieves' tools|phb}
Grinner.entries.0.items.1.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Grinner.entries.0.items.2.entry: A set of {@item fine clothes|phb}, a {@item disguise kit|phb}, a {@filter musical instrument|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=instrument} of your choice, a gold-plated ring depicting a smiling face, and a {@item pouch|phb} containing 15 gp
Grinner.entries.0.items.2.name: Equipment:
Grinner.entries.1.entries.0: Like every Grinner, you know how to find a hideout. In any city of 10,000 people or more on the Menagerie Coast or in the lands of the Dwendalian Empire, you can play the "Ballad of the Grinning Fool" in a major tavern or inn. A member of the Golden Grin will find you and give shelter to you and any companions you vouch for. This shelter might be discontinued if it becomes too dangerous to hide you, at the DM's discretion.
Grinner.entries.1.entries.1: This feature must be used with caution, for not all who know the ballad are your friends. Some are traitors, counterspies, or agents of tyranny.
Grinner.entries.1.name: Feature: Ballad of the Grinning Fool
Grinner.entries.2.entries.0: Grinners are trained in the art of secrecy and innuendo, and are skilled at hiding in plain sight by being the loudest and brightest person in the room. Their skills in subterfuge and combat lend themselves well to an adventuring lifestyle, and traveling with mercenaries and treasure hunters creates a convenient excuse to journey through lands bent under tyranny.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.1.rows.0.1: I love the spotlight. Everyone, look at me!
Grinner.entries.2.entries.1.rows.1.1: Give me a drink and I'm your friend.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.1.rows.2.1: Talk to me about yourself. I'm a hell of a listener.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.1.rows.3.1: I hate to start fights, but I love to finish them.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.1.rows.5.1: I'm always humming an old tune from my past.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.1.rows.6.1: When I don't have a reason to smile, I'm miserable.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.1.rows.7.1: I'm lucky like you wouldn't believe.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.2.rows.0.1: Revolution. Tyrants must fall, no matter the cost. (Chaotic)
Grinner.entries.2.entries.2.rows.1.1: Compassion. The only way to make a better world is to perform small kindnesses. (Good)
Grinner.entries.2.entries.2.rows.2.1: Justice. A nation built upon just foundations will uphold freedom for all. (Law)
Grinner.entries.2.entries.2.rows.3.1: Expression. Music, joy, and laughter are the keys to freedom. (Good)
Grinner.entries.2.entries.2.rows.4.1: Self-Determination. People should be free to do as they please. (Chaotic)
Grinner.entries.2.entries.2.rows.5.1: Vigilance. A free people must be carefully taught, lest they be misled. (Neutral)
Grinner.entries.2.entries.3.rows.0.1: I lost someone important to an agent of the Dwendalian Empire. That regime will fall.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.3.rows.1.1: The first people to be hurt by this war will be the common folk. I need to protect them.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.3.rows.2.1: Music helped me through a dark time in my life. Now, I'll use music to change the world.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.3.rows.3.1: I will be known as the greatest spy who ever lived.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.3.rows.4.1: All life is precious to me. I know I can change the world without taking a humanoid life.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.3.rows.5.1: The elite in their ivory towers don't understand how we suffer. I intend to show them.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.4.rows.0.1: I've never lied once in my life. What? No, I'm not crossing my fingers!
Grinner.entries.2.entries.4.rows.1.1: I do everything big! Subtlety? I don't know the meaning of subtlety! Oh, that's a problem?
Grinner.entries.2.entries.4.rows.2.1: Being a spy in wartime is painful. I've seen so much suffering, I think I'm losing my mind.
Grinner.entries.2.entries.4.rows.3.1: I can't focus on my mission. I just want to carouse and sing and play!
Grinner.entries.2.entries.4.rows.4.1: Yeah, that's my name. Yeah, I'm a Grinner spy. Who cares about staying undercover?
Grinner.entries.2.entries.4.rows.5.1: I can't afford to trust anyone. Not. Anyone.
Grinner.entries.2.name: Suggested Characteristics
Grinner.name: Grinner
Gruul Anarch.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Gruul Anarch.entries.0.items.1.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Gruul Anarch.entries.0.items.2.entry: Choose one of Draconic, Giant, Goblin, or Sylvan
Gruul Anarch.entries.0.items.2.name: Languages:
Gruul Anarch.entries.0.items.3.entry: A Gruul insignia, a {@item hunting trap|phb}, an {@item herbalism kit|phb}, the skull of a boar, a beast-hide cloak, a set of {@item traveler's clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 10 gp (Azorius 1-zino coins)
Gruul Anarch.entries.0.items.3.name: Equipment:
Gruul Anarch.entries.1.entries.0: You are intimately familiar with areas of the city that most people shun: ruined neighborhoods where wurms rampaged, overgrown parks that no hand has tended in decades, and the vast, sprawling rubblebelts of broken terrain that civilized folk have long abandoned. You can find a suitable place for you and your allies to hide or rest in these areas. In addition, you can find food and fresh water in these areas for yourself and up to five other people each day.
Gruul Anarch.entries.1.name: Feature: Rubblebelt Refuge
Gruul Anarch.entries.2.entries.0: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature}
Gruul Anarch.entries.2.entries.1: For you, the spells on the Gruul Guild Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class. (If you are a multiclass character with multiple spell lists, these spells are added to all of them.)
Gruul Anarch.entries.2.entries.2.caption: Gruul Guild Spells
{@spell compelled duel}, {@spell speak with animals}, {@spell thunderwave}
Gruul Anarch.entries.2.entries.3: Fueled by the fire of rage burning in your heart, your magic is almost always accompanied by fiery effects, such as flames smoldering behind your eyes or dancing over your hands.
Gruul Anarch.entries.2.name: Gruul Guild Spells
Gruul Anarch.entries.3.entries.0: Gruul ways aren't the ways of civilized folk, and the Gruul have little patience for social niceties. But they do have cherished traditions and values, just as important to them as the different values held by the urban, cosmopolitan culture of Ravnica.
Gruul Anarch.entries.3.entries.1.caption: Personality Traits
Unlike people, the beasts of the wild are friends who won't stab me in the back.
Go ahead and insult me—I dare you.
I scorn those who can't survive away from the comforts of the city.
Don't tell me I'm not allowed to do something.
Laws are for people who are afraid to face their inner beasts.
I smear the blood of my enemies over my skin.
I was, in fact, raised by maaka.
HarrRRAAGGHH! [I rarely form a coherent sentence and prefer to express myself by breaking things.]
Gruul Anarch.entries.3.entries.2.caption: Ideals
{@b Clan}. My clan is all that really matters. (Any)
{@b Anarchy}. No person or law or custom can tell another what to do. (Chaotic)
{@b Nature}. We weren't born tame or domesticated, so we shouldn't have to live that way. (Neutral)
{@b Might}. The strongest are meant to dominate the weak. (Evil)
{@b Rage}. AAAAAARRRRggggh! [To live is to feel and express the rage burning in your belly.] (Chaotic)
{@b Tradition}. The Old Ways must be preserved and upheld. (Any)
Gruul Anarch.entries.3.entries.3.caption: Bonds
I am determined that one day I will lead my clan—or a new one.
I would give my life for my clan chieftain.
The chieftain of another clan has a grudge against me.
I am devoted to a sacred site in the midst of the rubblebelt.
My weapon is made from the first raktusk I ever hunted.
GrrrRRAAAAGGHH! [I will do anything to prove myself greater than my siblings or ancestors.]
Gruul Anarch.entries.3.entries.4.caption: Flaws
If you question my courage, I will never back down.
HrrrGGGAAAARRuuuh! [My anger in battle led to the death of a loved one.]
I'm as stubborn as a batterboar.
I'm so convinced of my superiority over soft, civilized people that I'll take great risks to prove it.
I'm easily manipulated by people I find attractive.
I'm not actually all that angry.
Gruul Anarch.entries.3.name: Suggested Characteristics
Gruul Anarch.entries.4.entries.0: The members of the Gruul Clans rely on each other even as they vie for territory and glory. Their encounters with members of other guilds are more often violent than friendly, but occasional bonds do form.
Gruul Anarch.entries.4.entries.1: Roll twice on the Gruul Contacts table (for an ally and a rival) and once on the Non-Gruul Contacts table.
Gruul Anarch.entries.4.entries.2.caption: Gruul Contacts
One of my parents is a renowned warrior in my clan.
My sibling has the ear of the clan chief.
I have cousins in a different clan.
When we were younger, I was romantically involved with a prominent warrior in my clan.
A druid in my clan believes I have a destiny to fulfill.
The warrior who trained me remembers me for my exceptional potential.
My clan chief killed one of my parents, who had challenged the chief for leadership of the clan. Some combination of resentment and remorse stirs the clan chief to help me sometimes.
I made a strong impression on Borborygmos.
Gruul Anarch.entries.4.entries.3.caption: Non-Gruul Contacts
An Azorius arrester thinks I can be reformed.
A Boros soldier gives me gifts in exchange for information about other clans' movements.
I once caught and released a Dimir spy.
I consult with a Golgari shaman for spiritual guidance at times.
Roll an additional Gruul contact; you can decide if the contact is an ally or a rival.
An Izzet scientist blames the Gruul for the destruction of his life's work in a raid, but seems to think that I'm not like other Gruul.
I foolishly borrowed money from an Orzhov syndic to indulge a shameful vice.
A close friend left our clan and joined the Cult of Rakdos.
A distant relative is trying to recruit me into the Selesnya Conclave.
I stopped a Simic biomancer from trapping wild beasts to perform vile experiments on them.
Gruul Anarch.entries.4.name: Contacts
Gruul Anarch.entries.5.entries.0: In service of a simple goal, you have a simple part to play: Fight. Unleash your rage. Flatten buildings and defeat those who stand in your way. Be Gruul, in your own way.
Gruul Anarch.entries.5.entries.1: You will frequently be summoned to participate in a raid your clan is launching against the city or against a group of its defenders. Your clan leader might also send you on a special mission, though it would almost certainly still qualify as a raid. You might join a small group of Gruul warriors on a dangerous charge deep into the settled streets to plunder a certain location, retrieve an item stolen from your clan, or assault a Boros garrison.
Gruul Anarch.entries.5.entries.2: Sometimes your objective might be more esoteric. With prophecies of the return of Ilharg the Raze-Boar spreading like wildfire among the Gruul druids, you might be asked to carry out some task that the druids believe will speed his coming. Such a task might involve collecting a sacred relic held in an Orzhov vault or collecting sacrifices for a grand ceremony in the Raze-Boar's honor.
Gruul Anarch.entries.5.name: How Do I Fit In?
Gruul Anarch.name: Gruul Anarch
Guard.entries.0.items.0.name: Ability Scores:
Guard.entries.0.items.1.name: Feat:
Guard.entries.0.items.2.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Guard.entries.0.items.3.entry: Choose one kind of {@item Gaming Set|XPHB}
Guard.entries.0.items.3.name: Tool Proficiency:
Guard.entries.0.items.4.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Spear|XPHB}, {@item Light Crossbow|XPHB}, 20 {@item Bolt|XPHB|Bolts}, {@item Gaming Set|XPHB} (same as above), {@item Hooded Lantern|XPHB}, {@item Manacles|XPHB}, {@item Quiver|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 12 GP; or (B) 50 GP
Guard.entries.0.items.4.name: Equipment:
Guard.fluff.0: Your feet ache when you remember the countless hours you spent at your post in the tower. You were trained to keep one eye looking outside the wall, watching for marauders sweeping from the nearby forest, and your other eye looking inside the wall, searching for cutpurses and troublemakers.
Guard.name: Guard
Guide.entries.0.items.0.name: Ability Scores:
Guide.entries.0.items.1.name: Feat:
Guide.entries.0.items.2.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Guide.entries.0.items.3.name: Tool Proficiency:
Guide.entries.0.items.4.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Shortbow|XPHB}, {@item Arrow|XPHB|20 Arrows}, {@item Cartographer's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Bedroll|XPHB}, {@item Quiver|XPHB}, {@item Tent|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 3 GP; or (B) 50 GP
Guide.entries.0.items.4.name: Equipment:
Guide.fluff.0: You came of age outdoors, far from settled lands. Your home was anywhere you chose to spread your bedroll. There are wonders in the wilderness—strange monsters, pristine forests and streams, overgrown ruins of great halls once trod by giants—and you learned to fend for yourself as you explored them. From time to time, you guided friendly nature priests who instructed you in the fundamentals of channeling the magic of the wild.
Guide.name: Guide
Guild Artisan.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Guild Artisan.entries.0.items.1.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Guild Artisan.entries.0.items.2.name: Languages:
Guild Artisan.entries.0.items.3.entry: A set of {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools} (one of your choice), a letter of introduction from your guild, a set of {@item traveler's clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 15 gp
Guild Artisan.entries.0.items.3.name: Equipment:
Guild Artisan.entries.1.entries.0: As an established and respected member of a guild, you can rely on certain benefits that membership provides. Your fellow guild members will provide you with lodging and food if necessary, and pay for your funeral if needed. In some cities and towns, a guildhall offers a central place to meet other members of your profession, which can be a good place to meet potential patrons, allies, or hirelings.
Guild Artisan.entries.1.entries.1: Guilds often wield tremendous political power. If you are accused of a crime, your guild will support you if a good case can be made for your innocence or the crime is justifiable. You can also gain access to powerful political figures through the guild, if you are a member in good standing. Such connections might require the donation of money or magic items to the guild's coffers.
Guild Artisan.entries.1.entries.2: You must pay dues of 5 gp per month to the guild. If you miss payments, you must make up back dues to remain in the guild's good graces.
Guild Artisan.entries.1.name: Feature: Guild Membership
Guild Artisan.entries.2.entries.0: Guilds are generally found in cities large enough to support several artisans practicing the same trade. However, your guild might instead be a loose network of artisans who each work in a different village within a larger realm. Work with your DM to determine the nature of your guild. You can select your guild business from the Guild Business table or roll randomly.
Guild Artisan.entries.2.name: Specialty
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.0: Guild artisans are among the most ordinary people in the world—until they set down their tools and take up an adventuring career. They understand the value of hard work and the importance of community, but they're vulnerable to sins of greed and covetousness.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.1.rows.0: I believe that anything worth doing is worth doing right. I can't help it—I'm a perfectionist.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.1.rows.1: I'm a snob who looks down on those who can't appreciate fine art.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.1.rows.2: I always want to know how things work and what makes people tick.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.1.rows.3: I'm full of witty aphorisms and have a proverb for every occasion.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.1.rows.4: I'm rude to people who lack my commitment to hard work and fair play.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.1.rows.5: I like to talk at length about my profession.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.1.rows.6: I don't part with my money easily and will haggle tirelessly to get the best deal possible.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.1.rows.7: I'm well known for my work, and I want to make sure everyone appreciates it. I'm always taken aback when people haven't heard of me.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.2.rows.0: Community. It is the duty of all civilized people to strengthen the bonds of community and the security of civilization. (Lawful)
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.2.rows.1: Generosity. My talents were given to me so that I could use them to benefit the world. (Good)
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.2.rows.2: Freedom. Everyone should be free to pursue his or her own livelihood. (Chaotic)
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.2.rows.3: Greed. I'm only in it for the money. (Evil)
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.2.rows.4: People. I'm committed to the people I care about, not to ideals. (Neutral)
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.2.rows.5: Aspiration. I work hard to be the best there is at my craft. (Any)
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.3.rows.0: The workshop where I learned my trade is the most important place in the world to me.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.3.rows.1: I created a great work for someone, and then found them unworthy to receive it. I'm still looking for someone worthy.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.3.rows.2: I owe my guild a great debt for forging me into the person I am today.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.3.rows.3: I pursue wealth to secure someone's love.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.3.rows.4: One day I will return to my guild and prove that I am the greatest artisan of them all.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.3.rows.5: I will get revenge on the evil forces that destroyed my place of business and ruined my livelihood.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.4.rows.0: I'll do anything to get my hands on something rare or priceless.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.4.rows.1: I'm quick to assume that someone is trying to cheat me.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.4.rows.2: No one must ever learn that I once stole money from guild coffers.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.4.rows.3: I'm never satisfied with what I have—I always want more.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.4.rows.4: I would kill to acquire a noble title.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.entries.4.rows.5: I'm horribly jealous of anyone who can outshine my handiwork. Everywhere I go, I'm surrounded by rivals.
Guild Artisan.entries.3.name: Suggested Characteristics
Guild Artisan.name: Guild Artisan
Harborfolk.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Harborfolk.entries.0.items.1.entry: One type of {@filter gaming set|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=gaming set}, {@filter vehicles (water)|items|source=phb;dmg|miscellaneous=mundane|type=vehicle (water)}
Harborfolk.entries.0.items.1.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Harborfolk.entries.0.items.2.entry: {@item Fishing tackle|phb}, {@item dice set|phb}, {@item playing card set|phb}, or {@item Three-Dragon Ante set|phb}, a set of {@item common clothes|phb}, {@item rowboat}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 5 gp
Harborfolk.entries.0.items.2.name: Equipment:
Harborfolk.entries.1.name: Lifestyle
Harborfolk.entries.2.entries.0: You are one of the hundreds of small-time fishermen and women who haul the bounty of Mulmaster's freshwater harbor to the city's markets each morning. You have spent countless days rowing in the waters in and around Mulmaster and know them and the other fisherfolk, dockworkers, and port inhabitants better than anyone. Though you have left that life behind, you still visit once in a while.
Harborfolk.entries.2.name: Overview
Harborfolk.entries.3.entries.0: You grew up on the docks and waters of Mulmaster Harbor. The harborfolk remember you and still treat you as one of them. They welcome you and your companions. While they might charge you for it, they'll always offer what food and shelter they have; they'll even hide you if the City Watch is after you (but not if the Hawks are). Note: This feature is a variant of the Folk Hero feature.
Harborfolk.entries.3.name: Feature: Harborfolk
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.0.rows.0.1: I am curious. I want to know why things are the way they are and why people do the things that they do.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.0.rows.1.1: I can't sing, but that never stops me from doing it, loudly. Everyone loves a good sea chanty!
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.0.rows.2.1: I think the High Blade is doing a terrific job, don't you?
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.0.rows.3.1: I'm very excited that the House Built on Gold is being restored. I am a zealous worshipper of Waukeen.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.0.rows.4.1: I am quite superstitious. I see portents in everyday occurrences.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.0.rows.5.1: I resent the rich and enjoy thwarting their plans and spoiling their fun in small ways.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.0.rows.6.1: I have a sea story to fit every occasion.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.0.rows.7.1: I'm a fisher, but I secretly detest eating fish. I will do anything to avoid it.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.1.rows.0.1: Calm. For all things, there is a tide. I set sail when it is right, and mend my nets when it is not. (Lawful)
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.1.rows.1.1: Windblown. I go where the winds blow. No man or woman tells me where or when to sail. (Chaotic)
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.1.rows.2.1: Aspiring. I will gain the favor of a Zor or Zora patron, maybe even one of the Blades! (Any)
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.1.rows.3.1: Salty. I want people to look to me as an expert on plying Mulmaster Harbor. (Any)
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.1.rows.4.1: Selfless. We are all children of the sea. I help everyone in peril afloat and ashore. (Good)
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.1.rows.5.1: Let them Drown. I refuse to risk my hide to help others. They wouldn't help me if roles were reversed. (Evil)
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.2.rows.0.1: I once lost everything but my rowboat. I'll do anything to protect it.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.2.rows.1.1: My brother was in the Soldiery, but he was killed. I really look up to the men and women who serve.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.2.rows.2.1: The Cloaks killed my friend for spellcasting. I'll get them back somehow, someday.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.2.rows.3.1: The High House of Hurting helped me when I was hurt and asked nothing in return. I owe them my life.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.2.rows.4.1: I was robbed in the Zhent ghetto once. It will not happen again.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.2.rows.5.1: I would do anything to protect the other harborfolk. They are my family.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.3.rows.0.1: I drink too much, which causes me to miss the tide.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.3.rows.1.1: I killed a drunk member of the City Watch in a brawl. I am terrified that they might find out.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.3.rows.2.1: I oversell myself and make promises I can't keep when I want to impress someone.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.3.rows.3.1: Book learning is a waste of time. I have no patience for people who don't speak from experience.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.3.rows.4.1: I almost always cheat. I can't help myself.
Harborfolk.entries.4.entries.3.rows.5.1: I am a secret informant for the Hawks. I send them reports about everything I see and hear, even what my friends and allies are up to.
Harborfolk.entries.4.name: Suggested Characteristics
Harborfolk.name: Harborfolk
Harper.entries.0.items.0.name: Ability Scores:
Harper.entries.0.items.1.name: Feat:
Harper.entries.0.items.2.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Harper.entries.0.items.3.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Harper.entries.0.items.4.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}, {@item Bedroll|XPHB}, {@item Costume|XPHB}, {@item Grappling Hook|XPHB}, {@item Rope|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 14 GP; or (B) 50 GP
Harper.entries.0.items.4.name: Equipment:
Harper.fluff.0: You accepted an invitation to join the Harpers, pledging an oath to uphold the Harper code and act in service to the common good. Like all Harpers, you understand the value of teamwork as well as when it's best to go it alone. Harper veterans have taught you the order's secrets—magical melodies, special watchwords, and legerdemain—and have entrusted you to use such knowledge to surveil and undermine the forces of evil.
Harper.name: Harper
Heretic.entries.0.items.0.name: Ability Scores:
Heretic.entries.0.items.1.name: Feat:
Heretic.entries.0.items.2.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Heretic.entries.0.items.3.name: Tool Proficiency:
Heretic.entries.0.items.4.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}, Heretical Text, {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 10 GP; or (B) 50 GP
Heretic.entries.0.items.4.name: Equipment:
Heretic.name: Heretic
Hermit.entries.0.items.0.name: Ability Scores:
Hermit.entries.0.items.1.name: Feat:
Hermit.entries.0.items.2.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Hermit.entries.0.items.3.name: Владение инструментами:
Hermit.entries.0.items.4.entry: Выберите А или Б: (А) {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB|Боевой посох}, {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB|Набор травника}, {@item Bedroll|XPHB|Спальник}, {@item Book|XPHB|Книга} (философия), {@item Lamp|XPHB|Лампа}, {@item Oil|XPHB|Масло} (3 фляги), {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB|Дорожная одежда}, 16 ЗМ; или (Б) 50 ЗМ
Hermit.entries.0.items.4.name: Экипировка:
Hermit.fluff.0: Вы провели свои ранние годы в одиночестве, в хижине или монастыре, далеко за пределами ближайшего поселения. В те дни единственными вашими спутниками были лесные обитатели и те, кто время от времени навещал вас, принося новости о внешнем мире и припасы. В уединении вы провели много часов, размышляя о тайнах сотворения мира.
Hermit.name: Отшельник
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.0.items.1.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.0.items.2.entr: A set of {@item fine clothes|phb}, a {@item signet ring|phb}, a letter of introduction from your family's trading house, and a purse containing 25 gp
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.0.items.2.name: Equipment:
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.1.name: Lifestyle
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.2.entries.0: Before becoming an adventurer, you were a successful merchant operating out Hillsfar, the City of Trade. Your family operated warehouses, organized caravans, managed stores, or owned a ship and has trade contacts throughout the Moonsea region, as well as up and down the length of the Sword Coast. Perhaps they import ore, uncut gems, untreated furs, or grain into the City of Trade, or they export fine cloth, faceted gems, fine furs, or Dragon's Breath, a brandy-like liquor. Regardless, you've largely given that life up for some reason and have chosen to seek adventure instead. Nevertheless, the training you received then, and perhaps the contacts you made, serve you well as an adventurer.
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.2.entries.1: Choose one of the following features:
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.2.name: Overview
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.3.entries.0: Although you've left the day-to-day life of a merchant behind, your family has assigned you the services of a loyal retainer from the business, a factor, husbanding agent, seafarer, caravan guard, or clerk. This individual is a commoner who can perform mundane tasks for you such as making purchases, delivering messages, and running errands. He or she will not fight for you and will not follow you into obviously dangerous areas (such as dungeons), and will leave if frequently endangered or abused. If he or she is killed, the family assigns you another within a few days. Note: This feature is a variant of the Noble Knight's Retainers feature.
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.3.name: Feature: Factor
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.4.entries.0: You and your family have trade contacts such as caravan masters, shopkeepers, sailors, artisans, and farmers throughout the Moonsea region and all along the Sword Coast. Once per game session, when adventuring in either of those areas, you can use those contacts to get information about the local area or to pass a message to someone in those areas, even across the great distance between the two areas. Note: This feature is a variant of the Criminal Contact and Researcher features.
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.4.name: Alternate Feature: Trade Contact
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.5.entries.0.ro: I fill my evenings with wine or mead and song.
I greatly admire gladiators and enjoy the Arena.
I take my wealth for granted. It seldom occurs to me that others aren't rich themselves.
I leave broken hearts all around the Moonsea and up and down the Sword Coast.
I work hard and seldom make time for fun.
I am a particularly devout and pray often.
The Red Plumes caught me once. I hate them.
I ask a lot of questions to get information about those with whom I am working and dealing.
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.5.entries.1.ro: Frugal. I spend my money very carefully. (Lawful)
Profligate. I tend to spend extravagantly. (Chaotic)
Honest. I deal with others above board. (Any)
Sharp. I seek to make the best deal possible. (Any)
Charitable. I give generously to others. (Good)
Greedy. I do not share my wealth with others. (Evil)
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.5.entries.2.ro: I am fiercely loyal to those with whom I work.
I must uphold the good name of my family.
I will prove myself to my family as an adventurer.
Deals are sacrosanct. I never go back on my word.
I love making deals and negotiating agreements.
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.5.entries.3.ro: I am a braggart. I promote myself shamelessly.
I am vain. I always wear the latest fashions.
I am a glutton. I eat and drink to excess.
I am a snob. I want only the finest things in life.
I am lazy. I want others to take care of everything.
I am overconfident. I overestimate my abilities.
Hillsfar Merchant.entries.5.name: Suggested Characteristics
Hillsfar Merchant.name: Hillsfar Merchant
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.0.items.0.name: Skill Proficiencies:
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.0.items.1.name: Languages:
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.0.items.2.name: Tool Proficiencies:
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.0.items.3.entr: A {@item forgery kit|phb}, a set of {@item common clothes|phb}, a belt {@item pouch|phb}, and 5 gp
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.0.items.3.name: Equipment:
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.1.name: Lifestyle
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.2.entries.0: Hillsfar is the City of Trade. However, the Great Law of Trade only protects "legitimate" trade, trade that passes through the city's sole gate, which the Red Plumes monitor and tax. And the Great Law of Humanity banishes non-humans from the city altogether. The two Great Laws create great demand and great risk for smugglers, who shepherd illicit goods and non-humans into and out of the city by secret routes. The Rogues Guild tightly controls all of this activity, taking its cut from sanctioned jobs and exacting punishment for independent jobs.
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.2.entries.1: Perhaps you trafficked Dragon's Breath (a brandy-like liquor) to avoid tariffs or contraband to avoid seizure, or maybe you are a human who sympathizes with the non-humans and worked as part of the network of secret routes and safe houses that helps them pass through Hillsfar. Either way, you have contacts in the smuggling community who can help you slip into and out of the city unnoticed, for a price.
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.2.name: Overview
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.3.entries.0: You can call on your contacts within the smuggling community to secure secret passage into or out of Hillsfar for yourself and your adventuring companions, no questions asked, and no Red Plume entanglements. Because you're calling in a favor, you can't be certain they will be able to help on your timetable or at all. Your Dungeon Master will determine whether you can be smuggled into or out of the city. In return for your passage, you and your companions may owe the Rouges Guild a favor and/or may have to pay bribes. Note: This feature is a variant of the Sailor feature.
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.3.name: Feature: Secret Passage
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.4.entries.0.ro: When I'm not smuggling, I gamble.
I just love Halfling cooking and baking!
I party with dwarves whenever I can.
I'm a terrible singer, but I love to do it.
I was raised to honor Chauntea and still do.
The blood sports of the Arena sicken me.
I think non-humans are really interesting.
I exaggerate the tales of my exploits.
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.4.entries.1.ro: Fair. I think everyone deserves to be treated fairly. I don't play favorites. (Lawful)
Impulsive. Planning is often a waste of time. No plan survives contact with reality. It's easier to dive in and deal with the consequences. (Chaotic)
Curious. I want to learn as much as I can about the people and places I encounter. (Any)
Prepared. I think success depends on preparing as much as possible in advance. (Any)
Respectful. I think everyone deserves to be treated with respect and dignity, regardless of their race, creed, color, or origin. (Good)
Corrupt. I will break the law or act dishonestly if the money is right. (Evil)
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.4.entries.2.ro: I am loyal to the Rogues Guild and would do anything for them.
I love the city of Hillsfar and my fellow Hillsfarians, despite the recent problems.
I admire the elves. I help them whenever I can.
A gnome helped me once. I pay the favor forward.
I enjoy tricking the Red Plumes at every opportunity.
I smuggled agricultural goods for non-human farmers. I try to help them when I can.
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.4.entries.3.ro: My hatred for the Red Plumes burns so brightly that I have difficulty suppressing It around them.
The Red Plumes caught me once before, and I was branded for my crime. If they catch me again, for any offense, the punishment will be dire.
I treat all Hillsfarians poorly. I am disgusted with their failure to revolt against the Great Law of Humanity.
I have difficulty trusting strangers. Anyone could be a spy for the authorities.
I am greedy. There Isn't much I won't do for money.
I'm an informant for the Red Plumes. They let me continue my activities, so long as I pass them information about illegal activity in Hillsfar.
Hillsfar Smuggler.entries.4.name: Suggested Characteristics
Hillsfar Smuggler.name: Hillsfar Smuggler
House Agent.entries.0.items.0.name: Ability Scores:
House Agent.entries.0.items.1.name: Feat:
House Agent.entries.0.items.2.name: Skill Proficiencies:
House Agent.entries.0.items.3.entry: Choose one kind of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}
House Agent.entries.0.items.3.name: Tool Proficiencies:
House Agent.entries.0.items.4.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} (same as above), {@item Fine Clothes|XPHB}, 20 GP; or (B) 50 GP
House Agent.entries.0.items.4.name: Equipment:
House Agent.fluff.0: You are connected to one of the dragonmarked houses, but you haven't (yet) manifested a dragonmark. You might be a member of the family by birth or an employee of the house with no familial connection. You've earned your living by doing the business of the house, serving as the hands, feet, and eyes of house leadership in the world. You might have many old friends, mentors, and rivals in the house you serve, plus allies or enemies in other houses.
House Agent.name: House Agent
